title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capstone Defense · Docker Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares fresh daemon operations, upgrades, recovery, cleanup, orchestration boundary, and capstone defense decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for production Docker operations and release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
Plan Engine, API, containerd, runtime, BuildKit, Buildx, Compose, plugin, storage, and deprecated-feature compatibility across staged upgrade and rollback.
Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Daemon Config Live Restore
steps.instruction:
Order the Docker Daemon Config Live Restore container delivery evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Daemon Config Live Restore.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Docker Daemon Config Live Restore assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Daemon Config Live Restore, keep Docker Daemon Config Live Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
